attacking_style: Long-range attacks on climbs (13-17km from finish), exploiting summit crests or penultimate climbs rather than steep explosive finales. Prefers sustained power efforts over punchy accelerations, often from breakaways rather than GC groups.
team_tactics: Astana frequently places multiple riders in early breakaways to create launching platforms. When leading GC, team sets collective tempo to control without requiring personal attacks. Less effective when isolated without team support.
terrain_preference: Sustained climbing gradients where diesel engine excels; technical descents where he maintains/extends gaps; gravel sectors. Exposed on steep explosive finishes (Mur de Huy-style), final 300-500m punches, and requires marked others in reduced sprints.
vulnerability: Lacks explosive acceleration for steep finales (dropped/unable to respond within 500m multiple times). Compromised when mechanically isolated without teammates. Third-week Grand Tour fade pattern evident. Struggles to finish moves when repeatedly marked.
key_patterns: Wins via long solo efforts 13-17km out, maintaining gaps through technical descents (4 instances)
Attacks at/immediately after summit crests to exploit hesitation, not mid-climb explosions (3 instances)
Second places reveal insufficient final 300-500m punch despite perfect positioning (Strade Bianche, Fleche, Tour de Suisse)
Early breakaway positioning via team numerics, not attacking from GC groups (Tirreno stages demonstrate pattern)
Third-week Grand Tour decline: dropped/struggling multiple Giro week 3, abandoned Tour injured (5+ instances)
Exceptional cobbled/gravel handling for non-specialist, strongest GC rider on pavé sectors
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 showed late-career resurgence with consecutive Tour de Suisse podiums and strong ITT, but vulnerability to explosive attacks persists (Thomas finale drop). Peak 2019 form not replicated.
